---
title: "California Oil Well EDA"
author: "Lukas Lescano"
date: "2026-04-30"
format: pdf
---

# 1. Set up

## Packages

```{r}
#| label: packages
#| message: false

# general use and cleaning
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(snakecase)

# file organization
library(here)

# wrap axis labels
library(scales)

# reading .xlsx files
library(readxl)

# visualize missing data
library(naniar)

# arrange plots
library(patchwork)
```

# 2. Functions

```{r}
#| label: reading in data function

# Header is on row 2 in these CubeBrowser exports; row 1 is blank.
# Read everything as character first to avoid silent type coercion
read_cube <- function(path) {
  read_xlsx(path, skip = 1, col_types = "text",
            .name_repair = ~ ifelse(.x == "", "unknown_col", .x)) |>
    select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))
}

```

```{r}
#| label: inspect-fn

inspect_table <- function(df, name) {
  cat("==========================================\n")
  cat("TABLE:", name, "\n")
  cat("==========================================\n")
  cat("Dimensions:", nrow(df), "rows x", ncol(df), "cols\n\n")
  
  cat("--- Column names (raw) ---\n")
  print(names(df))
  
  cat("\n--- Per-column summary ---\n")
  summary_df <- tibble(
    column      = names(df),
    n_missing   = map_int(df, ~ sum(is.na(.x) | .x == "")),
    pct_missing = round(100 * n_missing / nrow(df), 1),
    n_unique    = map_int(df, ~ dplyr::n_distinct(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
    example_vals = map_chr(df, ~ {
      vals <- unique(na.omit(.x))
      vals <- vals[vals != ""]
      paste(head(vals, 3), collapse = " | ")
    })
  )
  print(summary_df, n = Inf)
  
  cat("\n--- First 5 rows ---\n")
  print(head(df, 5))
  
  cat("\n--- Duplicate full rows:", sum(duplicated(df)), "---\n")
  
  invisible(summary_df)
}
```

```{r}
#| label: clean cube function

clean_cube <- function(df) {
  df |>
    filter(!if_all(everything(), is.na)) |>   # drop fully blank separator rows
    fill(Project, Operator, `Supply Segment Group`, 
         `Life Cycle Category`, Longitude, Latitude,
         Country, `US State`, Year, Basin, .direction = "down") |>  # fill ALL group key columns
    clean_names()
}
```

# 3. Data

## Read in data

```{r}
#| label: data-raw
#| message: false

table1_raw <- read_cube(here("data", "CubeBrowserTableExport_2322B09F.xlsx"))
table2_raw <- read_cube(here("data", "CubeBrowserTableExport_75677428.xlsx"))
table3_raw <- read_cube(here("data", "CubeBrowserTableExport_41525648.xlsx"))
```

# 3. Inspecting data

```{r}
#| label: inspect-all

s1 <- inspect_table(table1_raw, "table1 (2322B09F)")
s2 <- inspect_table(table2_raw, "table2 (75677428)")
s3 <- inspect_table(table3_raw, "table3 (41525648)")
```

# 4. Differences and duplicates in tables

```{r}
#| label: checking for differences

# Compare column names across all three
names(table1_raw)
names(table2_raw)
names(table3_raw)

# Are they exactly the same?
identical(names(table1_raw), names(table2_raw))
identical(names(table1_raw), names(table3_raw))

# If not identical, find the differences
setdiff(names(table1_raw), names(table2_raw))
setdiff(names(table2_raw), names(table1_raw))
```

```{r}
#| label: duplicates

combined_raw <- bind_rows(
  table1_raw |> mutate(source_file = "2322B09F"),
  table2_raw |> mutate(source_file = "75677428"),
  table3_raw |> mutate(source_file = "41525648")
)

# How many full duplicate rows exist across files?
get_dupes(combined_raw)

# How many duplicates in table 1?
get_dupes(table1_raw)
```

# 5. Cleaning data

```{r}
#| label: clean cube

table1_clean <- clean_cube(table1_raw)
table2_clean <- clean_cube(table2_raw)
table3_clean <- clean_cube(table3_raw)
```
